library(DESeq2)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggpubr)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(phyloseq)

#Load R environment with our PhyloSeq object:
ps1 = readRDS(\/Users/maxmiao/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/Lankau_Lab/Ch3_Greenhouse_Exmpt_2018/Phyloseq_analysis/ps1_DESeq2_02232026.RDS\)
ps1_filt <- prune_samples(sample_sums(ps1) >= 8000, ps1)
sample_sums(ps1_filt)

```r
#Load R environment with our PhyloSeq object:
ps1 = readRDS(\/Users/maxmiao/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/Lankau_Lab/Ch3_Greenhouse_Exmpt_2018/Phyloseq_analysis/ps1_DESeq2_02232026.RDS\)
ps1_filt <- prune_samples(sample_sums(ps1) >= 8000, ps1)
sample_sums(ps1_filt)

#Aggregate counts based on taxa rank (in this case Family)
ps1_df = tax_glom(ps1_filt,taxrank=rank_names(ps1_filt)[2])
ps1_df = subset_taxa(ps1_df, Phylum != \NA\)
ps1_df = subset_samples(ps1_df, sample_sums(ps1_df) != 0)

#Now we want to separate based on Potato Clade (A, C, M)
ps1_df_A = subset_samples(ps1_df, Clade == 'A')
ps1_df_C = subset_samples(ps1_df, Clade == 'C')
ps1_df_NA = subset_samples(ps1_df, Clade == 'M')

#Convert Phyoseq objects into DESeq2 objects: 
dds1_A= phyloseq_to_deseq2(ps1_df_A, ~ Iteration)



<!-- rnb-output-end -->

```r
```r
#Aggregate counts based on taxa rank (in this case Family)
ps1_df = tax_glom(ps1_filt,taxrank=rank_names(ps1_filt)[2])
ps1_df = subset_taxa(ps1_df, Phylum != \NA\)
ps1_df = subset_samples(ps1_df, sample_sums(ps1_df) != 0)

#Now we want to separate based on Potato Clade (A, C, M)
ps1_df_A = subset_samples(ps1_df, Clade == 'A')
ps1_df_C = subset_samples(ps1_df, Clade == 'C')
ps1_df_NA = subset_samples(ps1_df, Clade == 'M')

#Convert Phyoseq objects into DESeq2 objects: 
dds1_A= phyloseq_to_deseq2(ps1_df_A, ~ Iteration)
```
```

<!-- rnb-source-end -->


<!-- rnb-output-end -->

<!-- rnb-text-begin -->


#Normalization step for our samples: 

Normalization using 'esimateSizeFactor' function: Accounts for variation in total read counts between samples so that counts can be comparable across samples without being biased by sequencing depth. We specify 'type = poscounts' for 16S data. Yields relative scaling and is ideal of compositional data like 16S amplicon sequencing. 



<!-- rnb-text-end -->


<!-- rnb-chunk-begin -->

-->

#Normalization step for our samples: 

# 1) Calculate geometic means for DESeq2 for normalization (use following function):
gm_mean = function(x, na.rm=TRUE){
  exp(sum(log(x[x > 0]), na.rm=na.rm) / length(x))
}

geoMeans_A = apply(counts(dds1_A), 1, gm_mean)
geoMeans_C = apply(counts(dds1_C), 1, gm_mean)
geoMeans_NA = apply(counts(dds1_NA), 1, gm_mean)

# 2) Normalization using 'esimateSizeFactor' function: Accounts for variation in total read counts between samples so that counts can be comparable across samples without being biased by sequencing depth. We specify GeoMeans to use genes with counts in all samples (or uses pseudocounts for taxon with zeros). Yields relative scaling and is ideal of compositional data like 16S amplicon sequencing. 

design(dds1_A) <- ~ 1 + Iteration
design(dds1_C) <- ~ 1 + Iteration
design(dds1_NA) <- ~ 1 + Iteration

dds2_A = estimateSizeFactors(dds1_A, geoMeans = geoMeans_A) 
dds2_C = estimateSizeFactors(dds1_C, geoMeans = geoMeans_C) 
dds2_NA = estimateSizeFactors(dds1_NA, geoMeans = geoMeans_NA) 

#Note: we wanted to provide a \frozen\ size factor calculcation for reproducibility. This function esimate the size factors 

#Re-rerun DESeq2 on subset data after data normalization: 
dds3_A = DESeq(dds2_A, fitType=\parametric\)



<!-- rnb-output-end -->

```r
```r
#Normalization step for our samples: 

# 1) Calculate geometic means for DESeq2 for normalization (use following function):
gm_mean = function(x, na.rm=TRUE){
  exp(sum(log(x[x > 0]), na.rm=na.rm) / length(x))
}

geoMeans_A = apply(counts(dds1_A), 1, gm_mean)
geoMeans_C = apply(counts(dds1_C), 1, gm_mean)
geoMeans_NA = apply(counts(dds1_NA), 1, gm_mean)

# 2) Normalization using 'esimateSizeFactor' function: Accounts for variation in total read counts between samples so that counts can be comparable across samples without being biased by sequencing depth. We specify GeoMeans to use genes with counts in all samples (or uses pseudocounts for taxon with zeros). Yields relative scaling and is ideal of compositional data like 16S amplicon sequencing. 

design(dds1_A) <- ~ 1 + Iteration
design(dds1_C) <- ~ 1 + Iteration
design(dds1_NA) <- ~ 1 + Iteration

dds2_A = estimateSizeFactors(dds1_A, geoMeans = geoMeans_A) 
dds2_C = estimateSizeFactors(dds1_C, geoMeans = geoMeans_C) 
dds2_NA = estimateSizeFactors(dds1_NA, geoMeans = geoMeans_NA) 

#Note: we wanted to provide a \frozen\ size factor calculcation for reproducibility. This function esimate the size factors 

#Re-rerun DESeq2 on subset data after data normalization: 
dds3_A = DESeq(dds2_A, fitType=\parametric\)
```
```

<!-- rnb-source-end -->


<!-- rnb-output-end -->

using pre-existing size factors estimating dispersions gene-wise dispersion estimates mean-dispersion relationship – note: fitType=‘parametric’, but the dispersion trend was not well captured by the function: y = a/x + b, and a local regression fit was automatically substituted. specify fitType=‘local’ or ‘mean’ to avoid this message next time. final dispersion estimates fitting model and testing

dds3_C = DESeq(dds2_C, fitType=\parametric\)



<!-- rnb-output-end -->

```r
```r
dds3_C = DESeq(dds2_C, fitType=\parametric\)
```

using pre-existing size factors estimating dispersions gene-wise dispersion estimates mean-dispersion relationship final dispersion estimates fitting model and testing – replacing outliers and refitting for 2 genes – DESeq argument ‘minReplicatesForReplace’ = 7 – original counts are preserved in counts(dds) estimating dispersions fitting model and testing

dds3_NA = DESeq(dds2_NA, fitType=\parametric\)



<!-- rnb-output-end -->

```r
```r
dds3_NA = DESeq(dds2_NA, fitType=\parametric\)
```

#Calculating coefficient vectors for DESeq2 output for each potato Clade
mod_mat = model.matrix(design(dds3_A), colData(dds3_A))

I1 <- colMeans(mod_mat[dds3_A$Iteration == \1\, ])
I3 <- colMeans(mod_mat[dds3_A$Iteration == \3\, ])
I5 <- colMeans(mod_mat[dds3_A$Iteration == \5\, ])

#And we can now define any contrasts we want:
##For Andigenum contrasts 
res1_I3_I1 <- results(dds3_A, contrast = I3 - I1)
res1_I5_I1 <- results(dds3_A, contrast = I5 - I1)
res1_I5_I3 <- results(dds3_A, contrast = I5 - I3)

```r
```r
#Calculating coefficient vectors for DESeq2 output for each potato Clade
mod_mat = model.matrix(design(dds3_A), colData(dds3_A))

I1 <- colMeans(mod_mat[dds3_A$Iteration == \1\, ])
I3 <- colMeans(mod_mat[dds3_A$Iteration == \3\, ])
I5 <- colMeans(mod_mat[dds3_A$Iteration == \5\, ])

#And we can now define any contrasts we want:
##For Andigenum contrasts 
res1_I3_I1 <- results(dds3_A, contrast = I3 - I1)
res1_I5_I1 <- results(dds3_A, contrast = I5 - I1)
res1_I5_I3 <- results(dds3_A, contrast = I5 - I3)

```
```

<!-- rnb-source-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiYGBgclxuI0dsdWUgcGh5bGEgY29sdW1uIHRvIERFU2VxMiBvYmplY3QgaW4gb3VyIHJlc3VsdHMgZGF0YWZyYW1lIGJhc2VkIG9uIHJvd25hbWUgaW4gYm90aCBvYmplY3RzIVxucmVzX1BoeWxhX0ExID0gY2JpbmQoYXMocmVzMV9JM19JMSwgXCJkYXRhLmZyYW1lXCIpLCBhcyh0YXhfdGFibGUocHMxX2RmX0EpW3Jvd25hbWVzKHJlczFfSTNfSTEpLCBdLCBcIm1hdHJpeFwiKSlcbiMgQWRkIHNpZ25pZmljYW5jZSBpbmRpY2F0b3JcbiAgcmVzX2RmIDwtIHJlc19QaHlsYV9BMSAlPiVcbiAgICBtdXRhdGUoU2lnbmlmaWNhbnQgPSBpZmVsc2UocGFkaiA8IDAuMDUsIFwiWWVzXCIsIFwiTm9cIiksIERpcmVjdGlvbiA9IGlmZWxzZShsb2cyRm9sZENoYW5nZSA+IDAsIFwiSW5jcmVhc2VkXCIsIFwiRGVjcmVhc2VkXCIpXG4gICAgKVxucmVzX2RmMSA9IGZpbHRlcihyZXNfZGYsIHJlc19kZiRTaWduaWZpY2FudCA9PSBcIlllc1wiKVxuXG5gYGAifQ== -->

```r
#Glue phyla column to DESeq2 object in our results dataframe based on rowname in both objects!
res_Phyla_A1 = cbind(as(res1_I3_I1, "data.frame"), as(tax_table(ps1_df_A)[rownames(res1_I3_I1), ], "matrix"))
# Add significance indicator
  res_df <- res_Phyla_A1 %>%
    mutate(Significant = ifelse(padj < 0.05, "Yes", "No"), Direction = ifelse(log2FoldChange > 0, "Increased", "Decreased")
    )
res_df1 = filter(res_df, res_df$Significant == "Yes")

```

<!-- rnb-output-end -->

<!-- rnb-chunk-end -->

<!-- rnb-text-end -->

<!-- rnb-source-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiYGBgclxucmVzX1BoeWxhX0EyID0gY2JpbmQoYXMocmVzMV9JNV9JMSwgXCJkYXRhLmZyYW1lXCIpLCBhcyh0YXhfdGFibGUocHMxX2RmX0EpW3Jvd25hbWVzKHJlczFfSTVfSTEpLCBdLCBcIm1hdHJpeFwiKSlcbiMgQWRkIHNpZ25pZmljYW5jZSBpbmRpY2F0b3JcbiAgcmVzX2RmMiA8LSByZXNfUGh5bGFfQTIgJT4lXG4gICAgbXV0YXRlKFNpZ25pZmljYW50ID0gaWZlbHNlKHBhZGogPCAwLjA1LCBcIlllc1wiLCBcIk5vXCIpLCBEaXJlY3Rpb24gPSBpZmVsc2UobG9nMkZvbGRDaGFuZ2UgPiAwLCBcIkluY3JlYXNlZFwiLCBcIkRlY3JlYXNlZFwiKVxuICAgIClcblxucmVzX2RmMiA9IGZpbHRlcihyZXNfZGYyLCByZXNfZGYyJFNpZ25pZmljYW50ID09IFwiWWVzXCIpXG5gYGAifQ== -->

```r
res_Phyla_A2 = cbind(as(res1_I5_I1, "data.frame"), as(tax_table(ps1_df_A)[rownames(res1_I5_I1), ], "matrix"))
# Add significance indicator
  res_df2 <- res_Phyla_A2 %>%
    mutate(Significant = ifelse(padj < 0.05, "Yes", "No"), Direction = ifelse(log2FoldChange > 0, "Increased", "Decreased")
    )

res_df2 = filter(res_df2, res_df2$Significant == "Yes")
```

<!-- rnb-source-end -->


<!-- rnb-output-end -->

<!-- rnb-chunk-end -->


<!-- rnb-text-begin -->



Now we want to overlay pA_1 over pA_2 for our final figure: 


<!-- rnb-text-end -->


<!-- rnb-chunk-begin -->

***

Chiletanum Differential abundance over time: 


<!-- rnb-text-end -->


<!-- rnb-chunk-begin -->


<!-- rnb-output-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiXG48IS0tIHJuYi1zb3VyY2UtYmVnaW4gZXlKa1lYUmhJam9pWUdCZ2NseHVJME5oYkdOMWJHRjBhVzVuSUdOdlpXWm1hV05wWlc1MElIWmxZM1J2Y25NZ1ptOXlJRVJGVTJWeE1pQnZkWFJ3ZFhRZ1ptOXlJR1ZoWTJnZ2NHOTBZWFJ2SUVOc1lXUmxYRzV0YjJSZmJXRjBJRDBnYlc5a1pXd3ViV0YwY21sNEtHUmxjMmxuYmloa1pITXpYME1wTENCamIyeEVZWFJoS0dSa2N6TmZReWtwWEc1Y2Jra3hJRHd0SUdOdmJFMWxZVzV6S0cxdlpGOXRZWFJiWkdSek0xOURKRWwwWlhKaGRHbHZiaUE5UFNCY0lqRmNJaXdnWFNsY2Jra3pJRHd0SUdOdmJFMWxZVzV6S0cxdlpGOXRZWFJiWkdSek0xOURKRWwwWlhKaGRHbHZiaUE5UFNCY0lqTmNJaXdnWFNsY2JrazFJRHd0SUdOdmJFMWxZVzV6S0cxdlpGOXRZWFJiWkdSek0xOURKRWwwWlhKaGRHbHZiaUE5UFNCY0lqVmNJaXdnWFNsY2JseHVJMEZ1WkNCM1pTQmpZVzRnYm05M0lHUmxabWx1WlNCaGJua2dZMjl1ZEhKaGMzUnpJSGRsSUhkaGJuUTZYRzVjYmlNalJtOXlJRU5vYVd4bGRHRnVkVzBnWTI5dWRISmhjM1J6SUZ4dWNtVnpNVjlKTTE5Sk1TQThMU0J5WlhOMWJIUnpLR1JrY3pOZlF5d2dZMjl1ZEhKaGMzUWdQU0JKTXlBdElFa3hLVnh1Y21Wek1WOUpOVjlKTVNBOExTQnlaWE4xYkhSektHUmtjek5mUXl3Z1kyOXVkSEpoYzNRZ1BTQkpOU0F0SUVreEtWeHVjbVZ6TVY5Sk5WOUpNeUE4TFNCeVpYTjFiSFJ6S0dSa2N6TmZReXdnWTI5dWRISmhjM1FnUFNCSk5TQXRJRWt6S1Z4dVhHNWdZR0FpZlE9PSAtLT5cblxuYGBgclxuI0NhbGN1bGF0aW5nIGNvZWZmaWNpZW50IHZlY3RvcnMgZm9yIERFU2VxMiBvdXRwdXQgZm9yIGVhY2ggcG90YXRvIENsYWRlXG5tb2RfbWF0ID0gbW9kZWwubWF0cml4KGRlc2lnbihkZHMzX0MpLCBjb2xEYXRhKGRkczNfQykpXG5cbkkxIDwtIGNvbE1lYW5zKG1vZF9tYXRbZGRzM19DJEl0ZXJhdGlvbiA9PSBcXDFcXCwgXSlcbkkzIDwtIGNvbE1lYW5zKG1vZF9tYXRbZGRzM19DJEl0ZXJhdGlvbiA9PSBcXDNcXCwgXSlcbkk1IDwtIGNvbE1lYW5zKG1vZF9tYXRbZGRzM19DJEl0ZXJhdGlvbiA9PSBcXDVcXCwgXSlcblxuI0FuZCB3ZSBjYW4gbm93IGRlZmluZSBhbnkgY29udHJhc3RzIHdlIHdhbnQ6XG5cbiMjRm9yIENoaWxldGFudW0gY29udHJhc3RzIFxucmVzMV9JM19JMSA8LSByZXN1bHRzKGRkczNfQywgY29udHJhc3QgPSBJMyAtIEkxKVxucmVzMV9JNV9JMSA8LSByZXN1bHRzKGRkczNfQywgY29udHJhc3QgPSBJNSAtIEkxKVxucmVzMV9JNV9JMyA8LSByZXN1bHRzKGRkczNfQywgY29udHJhc3QgPSBJNSAtIEkzKVxuXG5gYGBcblxuPCEtLSBybmItc291cmNlLWVuZCAtLT5cbiJ9 -->

#Calculating coefficient vectors for DESeq2 output for each potato Clade
mod_mat = model.matrix(design(dds3_C), colData(dds3_C))

I1 <- colMeans(mod_mat[dds3_C$Iteration == \1\, ])
I3 <- colMeans(mod_mat[dds3_C$Iteration == \3\, ])
I5 <- colMeans(mod_mat[dds3_C$Iteration == \5\, ])

#And we can now define any contrasts we want:

##For Chiletanum contrasts 
res1_I3_I1 <- results(dds3_C, contrast = I3 - I1)
res1_I5_I1 <- results(dds3_C, contrast = I5 - I1)
res1_I5_I3 <- results(dds3_C, contrast = I5 - I3)




<!-- rnb-output-end -->

<!-- rnb-source-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiYGBgclxuYGBgclxuI0NhbGN1bGF0aW5nIGNvZWZmaWNpZW50IHZlY3RvcnMgZm9yIERFU2VxMiBvdXRwdXQgZm9yIGVhY2ggcG90YXRvIENsYWRlXG5tb2RfbWF0ID0gbW9kZWwubWF0cml4KGRlc2lnbihkZHMzX0MpLCBjb2xEYXRhKGRkczNfQykpXG5cbkkxIDwtIGNvbE1lYW5zKG1vZF9tYXRbZGRzM19DJEl0ZXJhdGlvbiA9PSBcXDFcXCwgXSlcbkkzIDwtIGNvbE1lYW5zKG1vZF9tYXRbZGRzM19DJEl0ZXJhdGlvbiA9PSBcXDNcXCwgXSlcbkk1IDwtIGNvbE1lYW5zKG1vZF9tYXRbZGRzM19DJEl0ZXJhdGlvbiA9PSBcXDVcXCwgXSlcblxuI0FuZCB3ZSBjYW4gbm93IGRlZmluZSBhbnkgY29udHJhc3RzIHdlIHdhbnQ6XG5cbiMjRm9yIENoaWxldGFudW0gY29udHJhc3RzIFxucmVzMV9JM19JMSA8LSByZXN1bHRzKGRkczNfQywgY29udHJhc3QgPSBJMyAtIEkxKVxucmVzMV9JNV9JMSA8LSByZXN1bHRzKGRkczNfQywgY29udHJhc3QgPSBJNSAtIEkxKVxucmVzMV9JNV9JMyA8LSByZXN1bHRzKGRkczNfQywgY29udHJhc3QgPSBJNSAtIEkzKVxuXG5gYGBcbmBgYCJ9 -->

```r
```r
#Calculating coefficient vectors for DESeq2 output for each potato Clade
mod_mat = model.matrix(design(dds3_C), colData(dds3_C))

I1 <- colMeans(mod_mat[dds3_C$Iteration == \1\, ])
I3 <- colMeans(mod_mat[dds3_C$Iteration == \3\, ])
I5 <- colMeans(mod_mat[dds3_C$Iteration == \5\, ])

#And we can now define any contrasts we want:

##For Chiletanum contrasts 
res1_I3_I1 <- results(dds3_C, contrast = I3 - I1)
res1_I5_I1 <- results(dds3_C, contrast = I5 - I1)
res1_I5_I3 <- results(dds3_C, contrast = I5 - I3)

```
```

<!-- rnb-source-end -->


<!-- rnb-output-end -->

<!-- rnb-chunk-end -->


<!-- rnb-text-begin -->


Plot contrast Chiletanum Iteration 3 vs Iteration 1

<!-- rnb-text-end -->


<!-- rnb-chunk-begin -->


<!-- rnb-output-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiXG48IS0tIHJuYi1zb3VyY2UtYmVnaW4gZXlKa1lYUmhJam9pWUdCZ2NseHVjbVZ6WDFCb2VXeGhJRDBnWTJKcGJtUW9ZWE1vY21Wek1WOUpNMTlKTVN3Z1hDSmtZWFJoTG1aeVlXMWxYQ0lwTENCaGN5aDBZWGhmZEdGaWJHVW9jSE14WDJSbVgwTXBXM0p2ZDI1aGJXVnpLSEpsY3pGZlNUTmZTVEVwTENCZExDQmNJbTFoZEhKcGVGd2lLU2xjYm1CZ1lDSjkgLS0+XG5cbmBgYHJcbnJlc19QaHlsYSA9IGNiaW5kKGFzKHJlczFfSTNfSTEsIFxcZGF0YS5mcmFtZVxcKSwgYXModGF4X3RhYmxlKHBzMV9kZl9DKVtyb3duYW1lcyhyZXMxX0kzX0kxKSwgXSwgXFxtYXRyaXhcXCkpXG5gYGBcblxuPCEtLSBybmItc291cmNlLWVuZCAtLT5cbiJ9 -->

res_Phyla = cbind(as(res1_I3_I1, \data.frame\), as(tax_table(ps1_df_C)[rownames(res1_I3_I1), ], \matrix\))



<!-- rnb-output-end -->

-->


#Prepare data from pC_1 and pC_2 
res_df1$Comparison <- \Iteration 3\
res_df2$Comparison <- \Iteration 5\

combined <- bind_rows(res_df1, res_df2)

#Only want to show statisticall significant bacterial families: 

families_keep <- combined %>%
  filter(Significant == \Yes\) %>%
  pull(Family) %>%
  unique()

combined_sig <- combined %>%
  filter(Family %in% families_keep)

#Fix Family Ordering: 

family_order <- res_df1 %>%
  filter(Significant == \Yes\) %>%
  arrange(log2FoldChange) %>%
  pull(Family)

combined_sig$Family <- factor(combined_sig$Family,
                              levels = family_order)

#Plot: 

Plot_C = ggplot(combined_sig,
       aes(x = log2FoldChange,
           y = Family,
           fill = Comparison)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = \dashed\) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.7),
           width = 0.6) +
  theme_classic()

Plot_C



<!-- rnb-output-end -->

```r
```r

#Prepare data from pC_1 and pC_2 
res_df1$Comparison <- \Iteration 3\
res_df2$Comparison <- \Iteration 5\

combined <- bind_rows(res_df1, res_df2)

#Only want to show statisticall significant bacterial families: 

families_keep <- combined %>%
  filter(Significant == \Yes\) %>%
  pull(Family) %>%
  unique()

combined_sig <- combined %>%
  filter(Family %in% families_keep)

#Fix Family Ordering: 

family_order <- res_df1 %>%
  filter(Significant == \Yes\) %>%
  arrange(log2FoldChange) %>%
  pull(Family)

combined_sig$Family <- factor(combined_sig$Family,
                              levels = family_order)

#Plot: 

Plot_C = ggplot(combined_sig,
       aes(x = log2FoldChange,
           y = Family,
           fill = Comparison)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = \dashed\) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.7),
           width = 0.6) +
  theme_classic()

Plot_C
```
```

<!-- rnb-chunk-end -->


<!-- rnb-text-begin -->


***
Contrast Matrix for North America across iteration 1 to iteration 5


<!-- rnb-text-end -->


<!-- rnb-chunk-begin -->

#Calculating coefficient vectors for DESeq2 output for each potato Clade
mod_mat = model.matrix(design(dds3_NA), colData(dds3_NA))

I1 <- colMeans(mod_mat[dds3_NA$Iteration == \1\, ])
I3 <- colMeans(mod_mat[dds3_NA$Iteration == \3\, ])
I5 <- colMeans(mod_mat[dds3_NA$Iteration == \5\, ])

#And we can now define any contrasts we want:

##For Chiletanum contrasts 
res1_I3_I1 <- results(dds3_NA, contrast = I3 - I1)
res1_I5_I1 <- results(dds3_NA, contrast = I5 - I1)
res1_I5_I3 <- results(dds3_NA, contrast = I5 - I3)



<!-- rnb-output-end -->

```r
```r
#Calculating coefficient vectors for DESeq2 output for each potato Clade
mod_mat = model.matrix(design(dds3_NA), colData(dds3_NA))

I1 <- colMeans(mod_mat[dds3_NA$Iteration == \1\, ])
I3 <- colMeans(mod_mat[dds3_NA$Iteration == \3\, ])
I5 <- colMeans(mod_mat[dds3_NA$Iteration == \5\, ])

#And we can now define any contrasts we want:

##For Chiletanum contrasts 
res1_I3_I1 <- results(dds3_NA, contrast = I3 - I1)
res1_I5_I1 <- results(dds3_NA, contrast = I5 - I1)
res1_I5_I3 <- results(dds3_NA, contrast = I5 - I3)
```
```

<!-- rnb-source-end -->


<!-- rnb-output-end -->

<!-- rnb-chunk-end -->


<!-- rnb-text-begin -->


Plot Contrast of N. American Iteration 3 vs Iteration 1

<!-- rnb-text-end -->


<!-- rnb-chunk-begin -->

<!-- rnb-source-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiYGBgclxucmVzX1BoeWxhID0gY2JpbmQoYXMocmVzMV9JM19JMSwgXCJkYXRhLmZyYW1lXCIpLCBhcyh0YXhfdGFibGUocHMxX2RmX05BKVtyb3duYW1lcyhyZXMxX0kzX0kxKSwgXSwgXCJtYXRyaXhcIikpXG4jIEFkZCBzaWduaWZpY2FuY2UgaW5kaWNhdG9yXG4gIHJlc19kZiA8LSByZXNfUGh5bGEgJT4lXG4gICAgbXV0YXRlKFNpZ25pZmljYW50ID0gaWZlbHNlKHBhZGogPCAwLjA1LCBcIlllc1wiLCBcIk5vXCIpLCBEaXJlY3Rpb24gPSBpZmVsc2UobG9nMkZvbGRDaGFuZ2UgPiAwLCBcIkluY3JlYXNlZFwiLCBcIkRlY3JlYXNlZFwiKVxuICAgIClcbiAgXG5yZXNfZGYxID0gZmlsdGVyKHJlc19kZiwgcmVzX2RmJFNpZ25pZmljYW50ID09IFwiWWVzXCIpXG5gYGAifQ== -->

```r
res_Phyla = cbind(as(res1_I3_I1, "data.frame"), as(tax_table(ps1_df_NA)[rownames(res1_I3_I1), ], "matrix"))
# Add significance indicator
  res_df <- res_Phyla %>%
    mutate(Significant = ifelse(padj < 0.05, "Yes", "No"), Direction = ifelse(log2FoldChange > 0, "Increased", "Decreased")
    )
  
res_df1 = filter(res_df, res_df$Significant == "Yes")
```

<!-- rnb-source-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiYGBgclxucmVzX1BoeWxhID0gY2JpbmQoYXMocmVzMV9JNV9JMSwgXCJkYXRhLmZyYW1lXCIpLCBhcyh0YXhfdGFibGUocHMxX2RmX05BKVtyb3duYW1lcyhyZXMxX0k1X0kxKSwgXSwgXCJtYXRyaXhcIikpXG4jIEFkZCBzaWduaWZpY2FuY2UgaW5kaWNhdG9yXG4gIHJlc19kZjIgPC0gcmVzX1BoeWxhICU+JVxuICAgIG11dGF0ZShTaWduaWZpY2FudCA9IGlmZWxzZShwYWRqIDwgMC4wNSwgXCJZZXNcIiwgXCJOb1wiKSwgRGlyZWN0aW9uID0gaWZlbHNlKGxvZzJGb2xkQ2hhbmdlID4gMCwgXCJJbmNyZWFzZWRcIiwgXCJEZWNyZWFzZWRcIilcbiAgICApXG5cbnJlc19kZjIgPSBmaWx0ZXIocmVzX2RmMiwgcmVzX2RmMiRTaWduaWZpY2FudCA9PSBcIlllc1wiKVxuYGBgIn0= -->

```r
res_Phyla = cbind(as(res1_I5_I1, "data.frame"), as(tax_table(ps1_df_NA)[rownames(res1_I5_I1), ], "matrix"))
# Add significance indicator
  res_df2 <- res_Phyla %>%
    mutate(Significant = ifelse(padj < 0.05, "Yes", "No"), Direction = ifelse(log2FoldChange > 0, "Increased", "Decreased")
    )

res_df2 = filter(res_df2, res_df2$Significant == "Yes")
```

-->

#Prepare data from pNA_1 and pNA_2 
res_df1$Comparison <- \Iteration 3\
res_df2$Comparison <- \Iteration 5\

combined <- bind_rows(res_df1, res_df2)

#Only want to show statisticall significant bacterial families: 

families_keep <- combined %>%
  filter(Significant == \Yes\) %>%
  pull(Family) %>%
  unique()

combined_sig <- combined %>%
  filter(Family %in% families_keep)

#Fix Family Ordering: 

family_order <- res_df1 %>%
  filter(Significant == \Yes\) %>%
  arrange(log2FoldChange) %>%
  pull(Family)

combined_sig$Family <- factor(combined_sig$Family,
                              levels = family_order)

#Plot: 

Plot_NA = ggplot(combined_sig,
       aes(x = log2FoldChange,
           y = Family,
           fill = Comparison)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = \dashed\) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.7),
           width = 0.6) +
  theme_classic()

Plot_NA

```r
```r
#Prepare data from pNA_1 and pNA_2 
res_df1$Comparison <- \Iteration 3\
res_df2$Comparison <- \Iteration 5\

combined <- bind_rows(res_df1, res_df2)

#Only want to show statisticall significant bacterial families: 

families_keep <- combined %>%
  filter(Significant == \Yes\) %>%
  pull(Family) %>%
  unique()

combined_sig <- combined %>%
  filter(Family %in% families_keep)

#Fix Family Ordering: 

family_order <- res_df1 %>%
  filter(Significant == \Yes\) %>%
  arrange(log2FoldChange) %>%
  pull(Family)

combined_sig$Family <- factor(combined_sig$Family,
                              levels = family_order)

#Plot: 

Plot_NA = ggplot(combined_sig,
       aes(x = log2FoldChange,
           y = Family,
           fill = Comparison)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = \dashed\) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.7),
           width = 0.6) +
  theme_classic()

Plot_NA
```
```

<!-- rnb-source-end -->

-->

library(cowplot)

Plot_A_modified = Plot_A + 
  theme(legend.position = \none\)+ 
        labs(title = \Andigenum\)

Plot_C_modified <- Plot_C + 
  theme(legend.position = \none\)+ 
        labs(title = \Chiletanum\)

Plot_NA_modified <- Plot_NA + 
  labs(title = \N. American\)


# Align plots vertically with same width
aligned_plots <- align_plots(Plot_A_modified,Plot_C_modified,Plot_NA_modified, align = \v\, axis = \l\, greedy = F)

# Plot grid with equal widths
plot_Fig3 = plot_grid(plotlist = aligned_plots, 
          ncol = 3, 
          nrow = 1,
          align = \h\,
          rel_heights = c(1, 1, 1))  # Adjust based on number of plots
plot_Fig3

```r
```r
library(cowplot)

Plot_A_modified = Plot_A + 
  theme(legend.position = \none\)+ 
        labs(title = \Andigenum\)

Plot_C_modified <- Plot_C + 
  theme(legend.position = \none\)+ 
        labs(title = \Chiletanum\)

Plot_NA_modified <- Plot_NA + 
  labs(title = \N. American\)


# Align plots vertically with same width
aligned_plots <- align_plots(Plot_A_modified,Plot_C_modified,Plot_NA_modified, align = \v\, axis = \l\, greedy = F)

# Plot grid with equal widths
plot_Fig3 = plot_grid(plotlist = aligned_plots, 
          ncol = 3, 
          nrow = 1,
          align = \h\,
          rel_heights = c(1, 1, 1))  # Adjust based on number of plots
plot_Fig3
```
```
